---
title: "TT"
author: "Mark Deming"
date: "2/03/2026"
output: html_document
---

Techniques learned from this TT were mainly data manipulation: use of `rowwise` and `mutate` to expand each row to include rows for each value in a sequence (see 67 and 77-78). Ridgelines plot itself is very similar to what I produced last week for Brazilian companies.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# Packages
pacman::p_load(here, tidyverse, usethis, gitcreds, tidytuesdayR, ggridges, extrafont)

# Open GitHub token page in browser
#usethis::create_github_token()

# Store the PAT securely
#gitcreds::gitcreds_set()

# Grab TT data
tt <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load("2026-02-03")

plants <- tt$edible_plants
head(plants)

# Relationship between water and temperature
plants %>%
  mutate(lower_temp = as.numeric(sub("-.*", "", temperature_growing)),
         upper_temp = as.numeric(sub(".*-", "", temperature_growing))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(lower_temp, water)) +
  geom_point()
  

# Relationship between water and temperature
plants %>%
  mutate(lower_temp = as.numeric(sub("-.*", "", temperature_growing)),
         upper_temp = as.numeric(sub(".*-", "", temperature_growing)),
         avg_temp = lower_temp + upper_temp / 2,
         water = case_when(water == "Very low" ~ "Very Low",
                           water == "very high" ~ "Very High",
                           water == "high" ~ "High",
                           .default = water), 
         water = factor(water, levels = c("Very Low", 
                                          "Low", 
                                          "Medium", 
                                          "High", 
                                          "Very High"))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(temperature_growing),
         !is.na(sunlight)) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_segment(aes(x = lower_temp, xend = upper_temp, y = reorder(common_name, avg_temp))) 


# Grab germination and grow temp ranges for each plant
plants <- plants %>%
  filter(!is.na(temperature_growing),
         !is.na(temperature_germination)) %>%
  mutate(grow_temp_lo = as.numeric(sub("-.*", "", temperature_growing)),
         grow_temp_hi = as.numeric(sub(".*-", "", temperature_growing)),
         germ_temp_lo = as.numeric(sub("-.*", "", temperature_germination)),
         germ_temp_hi = as.numeric(sub(".*-", "", temperature_germination))) 
  
# Expand each row into many using above ranges
expand_range <- function(lo, hi, step = 0.5) {
  seq(lo, hi, by = step)
}

temps_long <- plants %>%
  filter(!is.na(grow_temp_hi)) %>%
  select(taxonomic_name, common_name, cultivation,
         germ_temp_lo, germ_temp_hi,
         grow_temp_lo, grow_temp_hi) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    germ_temp = list(expand_range(germ_temp_lo, germ_temp_hi)),
    grow_temp = list(expand_range(grow_temp_lo, grow_temp_hi))
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  pivot_longer(c(germ_temp, grow_temp),
               names_to = "type",
               values_to = "temp") %>%
  unnest(temp) %>%
  mutate(
    type = recode(type,
                  germ_temp = "Germination",
                  grow_temp = "Growing")
  )

# Sanity check
temps_long %>%
  count(cultivation, type, name = "n_points") %>%
  arrange(type, desc(n_points))

# Designate a color palette
# Tableau palette with 14 colors
my_pal <- c("")

# Ridgelines plot
p <-
temps_long %>%
  group_by(type, cultivation) %>%
  mutate(median_temp = median(temp, na.rm = T)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = temp, y = reorder(cultivation, median_temp), fill = type)) +
  ggridges::geom_density_ridges(alpha = .6, color = "white", scale = 1.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Germination" = "#3B7A57",
                               "Growing" = "#E69F00"
)) +
    labs(x = "\nTemperature (Celsius)",
       y = "",
       title = "Growing and Germination Temperatures by Plant Family",
       fill = "Germination vs. Growing") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_line(linetype = "dotted", color = "gray70"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
        text = element_text(family = "Raleway"),
        plot.title.position = "plot",
        plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 25),
        legend.position = "top",
        legend.box.just = "left",
        legend.justification = c(0, 1),
        legend.box.margin = margin(l = -80))

# Save
ggsave(here("2026.02.03", "plant_growing_and_germination_temps.png"), 
       p,
       width = 8,
       height = 6)
```
